course.title: Digital Basics & Online Safety
course.description: A beginner-friendly, self-paced course that teaches safe and confident computer and internet use through clear lessons, real-life examples, and quick practice.

lessons.title: Lesson 1: What Is a Computer? Parts and Basic Use
lessons.explanation: A computer is a tool that helps you create, learn, and communicate. It has parts you can see, like the screen and keyboard, and parts inside that help it work.
keyPoints: Screen shows what the computer is doing
Keyboard and mouse are input tools
Turn off safely using the Shut Down option
examples: You use the mouse to open a learning website.
You type a message using the keyboard.
lessons.exercise: Identify the screen, keyboard, and mouse on your device and describe what each one does.

quiz.q: Which part shows pictures and text?
What is a safe way to turn off a computer?
The computer follows your instructions. True or false?

lessons.recap: You learned the basic parts of a computer and how to use them safely.
lessons.nextStep: Practice clicking and opening a simple app.

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Mouse, Keyboard, and Typing Practice
lessons.explanation: The mouse helps you point and click. The keyboard helps you type. Good control makes everything easier.
keyPoints: Left click selects, double click opens
Use the space bar and backspace when typing
examples: Double click a folder to open it.
Use backspace to fix a typing mistake.
lessons.exercise: Type your name and a short sentence. Then correct any mistakes using backspace.

quiz.q: What does a double click do?
Typing correctly is better than typing fast. True?

lessons.recap: You practiced mouse clicks and basic typing.
lessons.nextStep: Practice opening a document and typing a short paragraph.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Files, Folders, and Saving Work
lessons.explanation: Files are your documents, photos, and work. Folders help you organize them so you can find them quickly.
keyPoints: Know where your files are stored
examples: Save a homework file as: Math_Homework_1.
Create a folder called School to keep work together.
lessons.exercise: Create a folder named Learning and save a file inside it with your name.

quiz.q: What is a folder used for?
Which is a good file name?
You should know where your files are stored. True?

lessons.recap: You learned how to save and organize your work.
lessons.nextStep: Practice creating folders for different subjects.

lessons.title: Lesson 4: Internet Basics and Browsers
lessons.explanation: The internet is a network that connects people and information. A browser is the app you use to visit websites.
keyPoints: The address bar shows the website link
Use tabs to open multiple pages
Close tabs you do not need
examples: Open a browser and type a website address.
Use a new tab to open a second page.
lessons.exercise: Open your browser, visit a learning website, and open one new tab.

quiz.q: Where do you type a website link?

options: Close tabs you do not need

lessons.recap: You learned how to use a browser and tabs.
lessons.nextStep: Practice opening a trusted website and reading a short article.

lessons.title: Lesson 5: Searching Smartly
lessons.explanation: Search engines help you find information. Good keywords give better results and save time.
keyPoints: Check the website name before clicking
examples: Search: "how to create a folder".
Check if the website looks trustworthy.
lessons.exercise: Search for "basic computer lesson" and open two safe sources.

quiz.q: What should you check before clicking?

lessons.recap: You learned how to search safely and effectively.
lessons.nextStep: Practice finding a safe video lesson for beginners.

lessons.title: Lesson 6: Email and Messaging Safely
lessons.explanation: Email and messages help you communicate, but you must stay safe by checking who sent them.
keyPoints: Check the sender name and email
examples: An email asking for your password is a scam.
A teacher email uses the correct school address.
lessons.exercise: Write a short safe email greeting to a teacher or friend.

quiz.q: Should you share your password in email?
If a message looks strange you should

lessons.recap: You learned how to communicate safely.
lessons.nextStep: Practice identifying safe and unsafe emails.

lessons.title: Lesson 7: Passwords and Account Security
lessons.explanation: Strong passwords protect your accounts. Use unique passwords and never share them.
examples: A strong password looks like: Sun!River2026.
Use a password manager if needed.
lessons.exercise: Create a strong password example (do not use your real password).
lessons.recap: You learned how to create and protect strong passwords.
lessons.nextStep: Review your accounts and update weak passwords.

lessons.title: Lesson 8: Scams, Phishing, and Fake Links
lessons.explanation: Scammers trick people into sharing information. Learn to spot red flags and avoid harm.
examples: "Your account will close today" is a common scam line.
A fake website may have spelling mistakes.
lessons.exercise: Write three signs of a phishing message.
lessons.recap: You learned how to spot scams and fake links.
lessons.nextStep: Practice checking links before you click.

lessons.title: Lesson 9: Social Media Privacy and Digital Footprints
lessons.explanation: What you post online can stay forever. Protect your privacy and think before you share.
examples: Set your profile to private for better safety.
Do not share your home address in posts.
lessons.exercise: List three personal details you should never post publicly.

quiz.q: Personal info like address should be

lessons.recap: You learned how to protect privacy on social media.

lessons.title: Lesson 10: Safe Downloading and Device Protection
lessons.explanation: Downloads can bring helpful tools or harmful viruses. Only download from trusted sources.
examples: Download software from the official company website.
Ignore pop-up messages that offer random downloads.
lessons.exercise: Find the official website of a trusted app and identify the download button.
lessons.recap: You learned safe downloading and device protection.
lessons.nextStep: Review your device updates and security settings.

lessons.title: Lesson 11: Online Learning Tools
lessons.explanation: Online tools help you study, attend classes, and submit work. Learn the basics of common tools.
keyPoints: Use documents and slides for assignments
Share files only with trusted people
examples: Use a document tool to type homework.
Join class using a shared meeting link.
lessons.exercise: Create a folder called Online Learning and save a document in it.

quiz.q: Meeting links should be shared with

lessons.recap: You learned the basics of online learning tools.
lessons.nextStep: Practice joining a safe online class.

lessons.title: Lesson 12: Review and Final Safety Checklist
lessons.explanation: This final lesson reviews key safety rules and helps you build a daily safe-use habit.
examples: Check the website address before signing in.
Use a strong password and do not share it.
lessons.exercise: Write your personal online safety checklist with 5 rules.

quiz.q: Best habit before clicking a link?

lessons.recap: You reviewed all key safety skills for digital life.
lessons.nextStep: Apply your checklist every day online.
